# Dermot Crowley To Step Down As CEO Of Dalata Hotel Group

April 27, 2026 10:56 AM By Robert McHugh

Share this article

Share this article

Dermot Crowley has announced his intention to step down as chief executive officer of Dalata Hotel Group later this year, marking the end of a 13-year tenure with the company, including five years as CEO.

Crowley, who joined Dalata in 2012 as deputy CEO for Business Development & Finance, has played a central role in shaping the company’s growth trajectory.

He was instrumental in Dalata’s flotation in 2014 and led the transformational acquisition of the Moran Bewley Hotel Group in 2015, helping establish the business as a major player in the Irish and UK hotel markets.

During his time as deputy CEO, he oversaw significant expansion across Ireland and the UK, as well as guiding the group through the unprecedented challenges of the Covid-19 pandemic. In 2021, he stepped into the role of CEO, leading a new phase of growth and international development.

### 'Incredibly Proud'

“After 13 years with Dalata, I have decided that now is the right time for me to step away from the business," said Crowley.

ADVERTISEMENT

"I am incredibly proud of what we have achieved together as a team and especially since I took over as CEO in 2021. Over the last 5 years, we have added 16 hotels (3,800 rooms) across Ireland, the UK and Europe."

The news came after Dalata Hotel Group provided an update last week on how Dalata’s markets are planned to be led by Scandic Hotels Group, should Scandic seek to exercise an option to acquire Dalata’s hotel operations.

### "A Very Strong Place"

Pandox completed the acquisition of Dalata in November 2025. A management agreement subsequently took effect, under which Scandic assumed operational responsibility for Dalata’s hotels, while a business restructuring was initiated to separate Dalata’s property business from its hotel operations in line with Pandox’s business model.

The restructuring is currently anticipated to be completed in the second half of 2026, with a potential exercise of the option subsequent to this.

"The business is in a very strong place," said Crowley. "Through focusing on our people and our guests, our employee engagement and customer satisfaction scores are at record high levels.

"We are on target to open three new hotels in Dublin, Edinburgh and Berlin this year with a further four hotels in the pipeline in London, Edinburgh and Madrid. I have every confidence that under the leadership of Scandic, the appointed operator, Dalata will continue to prosper."

Dalata Executive: Leadership Behind Premier Hotel Experience

About Us

Executive Leadership Team

At Dalata, our people are at the heart of everything we do and our Executive Leadership Team leads by example. With deep experience across hotel operations, development, strategy, marketing, finance and people, they are united by a shared commitment to delivering brilliant experiences for our guests and creating opportunities for our teams. Together, they guide a growing, inclusive and ambitious business, focused on doing the right thing and bringing the heart of hospitality to life across Ireland, the UK and continental Europe.

###### Dermot Crowley

#### Dermot Crowley

Chief Executive Officer

Dermot was appointed as CEO in November 2021, having joined Dalata as Deputy CEO in 2012. He was instrumental in the IPO in 2014 and the €450m acquisition of the Moran Bewleys Hotels in 2015 and has led the development of the Group’s brands, the embedding of innovation culture and sustainability focus, and the significant acceleration in growth since his appointment as CEO.

Prior to joining Dalata, Dermot held senior positions in Ion Equity (and Irish Private Equity firm). Jurys Doyle hotel Group, Renault Ireland and Forte Hotels. From 2000 to 2006, he was Head of Acquisitions and Development of Jurys Doyle Hotel Group, where he led the rollout of the Jurys Inn brand in the UK.

Dermot is a Fellow of the Chartered Accountants Ireland, having trained in PwC, and holds a first-class honours Business degree from University College Cork.

×

###### Shane Casserly

#### Shane Casserly

Deputy Chief Executive Officer

Shane joined Dalata in 2014 as Head of Strategy and Development. He joined the board in January 2020 as Corporate Development Director and was appointed as Deputy CEO in September 2024. Shane has led the identification and delivery of key strategic asset acquisitions and developments for the company in Ireland and the UK, and has spearheaded Dalata’s move into Continental Europe.

Prior to joining Dalata, Shane held senior positions with private Equity firm Ion Equity, Jurys Doyle Hotel Group, Microsoft Europe and Musgrave Group. Within Jurys Doyle Hotel Group, from 2004 to 2007, his focus was on the rollout of the Jurys Inn brand in the UK, ultimately being appointed to Head of Acquisitions and Development in 2006.

Shane is a Fellow of the Chartered Accountants Ireland, having trained in PwC, and holds a Commerce degree from University College Cork.

×

###### Des McCann

#### Des McCann

Chief Operations Officer

Des joined Dalata in 2011 as a hotel General Manager. He was promoted to Group General Manager – Clayton Hotels in 2018 before being appointed as COO in January 2022. He was appointed to the Board of Dalata in January 2025. Since taking over as COO, Des has been instrumental in promoting a culture of innovation and driving operational efficiencies and enhancing guest experiences across the group’s properties

Prior to this, Des served as General Manager of the Clyde Hotel, The Ballsbridge Hotel, and Clayton Hotel Dublin Airport, bringing extensive hotel operations experience to his current position.

Des is a graduate of the National College of Ireland, has served on the Board of The Irish Hospitality Institute and is a former National Council member of the Irish Hotels Federation

×

###### Sean McKeon

#### Sean McKeon

Company Secretary and Head of Risk & Compliance

Sean joined Dalata in November 2007, shortly after the business was founded. He trained as a chartered accountant with Sligo-based Gilroy Gannon and over the following fifteen years worked with companies including Dunnes Stores, Keelings, Aer Rianta International and Diageo, based in Ireland, Spain and Russia. Sean is an MBA graduate of the UCD Michael Smurfit Graduate Business School.

×

###### Roma O’Connor

#### Roma O’Connor

Chief Marketing Officer

Roma was appointed as CMO in May 2022, when she joined Dalata. Roma has been instrumental in the brand repositioning of Dalata Hotel Group, Clayton Hotels and Maldron Hotels that took place in 2024, as well as driving the group’s digital transformation and implementing efficiencies across marketing processes. Prior to joining Dalata, Roma held senior marketing positions in the Bank of Ireland, McDonald’s, and GE, bringing over 20 years of experience with leading global brands. Roma holds an Honours Degree in Business from Dundee Business School and has served as the president of the Advertising Association of Ireland.

×

###### Dawn Wynne

#### Dawn Wynne

Chief People Officer

As Chief People Officer at Dalata Hotel Group, Dawn Wynne has been instrumental in building a high-performing, values-driven culture that underpins the company’s growth and success. Since joining Dalata in 2008, she has led transformative initiatives in talent development, leadership capability, and employee engagement, positioning Dalata as an employer of choice within the hospitality sector. A strategic and people-focused leader, Dawn is passionate about driving organisational excellence through the empowerment and development of teams, ensuring that Dalata’s people strategy continues to deliver sustainable business performance.

×

# Director Appointment and Board Committee Changes

RNS Number : 0658X

Dalata Hotel Group PLC

17 December 2019

Director Appointment and Board Committee Changes

ISE: DHG LSE: DAL

Dublin, 17 December 2019 | Dalata Hotel Group plc ("Dalata" or "the Company"), the largest hotel operator in Ireland, with a growing presence in the United Kingdom, is pleased to announce that Mr Shane Casserly has been appointed to the Board as Corporate Development Director; his appointment will be effective from 1 January 2020.

A graduate of University College Cork and Chartered Accountant, Mr Casserly joined Dalata in March 2014 as Head of Strategy and Development and has been instrumental in driving the Company's growth through acquisition and development activity in Ireland and the UK. He previously worked as Head of Development at Jurys Doyle Hotel Group plc and held senior positions with Ion Equity, Microsoft Europe and Musgrave Group.

The Company also announces a number of changes to its Committee structures and individual Non-executive Directors responsibilities which will also take effect from 1 January 2020.

A Committee with responsibility for oversight of Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) matters at the Company, has been formed and will be Chaired by Independent Non-Executive Director Elizabeth McMeikan. Ms McMeikan will be joined on the Committee by Robert Dix and Stephen McNally.

John Hennessy will assume the role of Chair of the Nomination Committee and Alf Smiddy will continue as Senior Independent Director and Non-executive Director with responsibility for workforce engagement. Elizabeth McMeikan will join the Remuneration Committee.

The revised composition of Board Committees is as follows:

John Hennessy, Chair, said:

"I am delighted to announce Shane Casserly's appointment to the Board as Corporate Development Director. Shane has been a leading member of the executive team since 2014 and his appointment will further strengthen the Board as the next phase of the Company's expansion into the UK gathers momentum.

The Board is very aware of the Company's responsibility for making a positive contribution to society, for managing its impact on the environment, and for governance matters. I believe that establishing a Board committee with specific responsibility for overseeing these efforts and reporting on them to the Board will provide leadership and demonstrate the priority given to ESG matters by Dalata"

Notes:

The Company confirms that there is no other information that is required to be disclosed in relation to Mr Casserly in accordance with rule 6.1.66 (1) to (6) of the Euronext Dublin Listing Rules and 9.6.13R(1) to (6) of the UK Listing Rules

ENDS

About Dalata

Dalata Hotel Group plc was founded in August 2007 and listed as a plc in March 2014. Dalata has a strategy of owning or leasing its hotels and also has a small number of management contracts. The Group's portfolio now consists of 30 owned hotels, 11 leased hotels and three management contracts with a total of 9,201 bedrooms. In addition to this, the Group is currently developing nine new hotels and has plans to extend two of its existing hotels, with a total of 2,399 bedrooms due to open over the next three years. This will bring the total number of bedrooms in Dalata to over 11,600. Dalata now has close to 5,000 employees. For the first six months of 2019, Dalata reported revenue of €201.9 million and a profit after tax of €32.7 million. Dalata is listed on the Main Market of Euronext Dublin (DHG) and the London Stock Exchange (DAL). For further information visit: www.dalatahotelgroup.com.

Subscribe &nbsp Tue 14 Apr, 2026

SUPPORTED BY

Appointment

Dalata Hotel Group

Appointment

Dalata Hotel Group appoints Shane Casserly as Deputy Chief Executive

Chief Operating Officer Des McCann will be appointed to the Board with effect from 1st January 2025

&nbspThursday 05th September 2024

Pictured: Shane Casserly, Deputy CEO and Dermot Crowley CEO.

---

Dalata Hotel Group is pleased to announce the appointment of Shane Casserly as Deputy Chief Executive. Mr. Casserly, who joined Dalata in March 2014 as Head of Strategy and Development, has been instrumental in driving the company’s growth through acquisition and development activities in Ireland and the UK. He was appointed to the Board as Corporate Development Director in January 2020.

Mr. Casserly’s extensive experience includes roles as Head of Development at Jurys Doyle Hotel Group plc and senior positions with Ion Equity, Microsoft Europe, and Musgrave Group.

Dalata is also pleased to announce that Chief Operating Officer Des McCann will be appointed to the Board with effect from 1st January 2025. Mr McCann joined the Group in 2011, and held General Manager positions at several hotels before he was appointed Group General Manager of Clayton Hotels in Ireland in November 2018. In January 2022, he was appointed Chief Operating Officer.

Dermot Crowley, Dalata Hotel Group CEO, commented, “I am very pleased to appoint Shane Casserly to the role of Deputy CEO. Shane was appointed to the Board in January 2020. I expanded Shane’s responsibility on taking over as CEO in November 2021 and the title of Deputy CEO more appropriately reflects his contribution across all elements of the business.

“I’m delighted with the appointment of Des McCann to the Board effective 1 January 2025. I appointed Des as COO in January 2022. He has made an outstanding contribution in the intervening period and will be a great addition to the Board. As I look ahead, I remain very confident on Dalata’s future growth prospects as we continue to deliver on our stated growth strategy, becoming a key four-star market player in targeted locations.”

---

Dalata Executive: Leadership Behind Premier Hotel Experience

About Us

Executive Leadership Team

At Dalata, our people are at the heart of everything we do and our Executive Leadership Team leads by example. With deep experience across hotel operations, development, strategy, marketing, finance and people, they are united by a shared commitment to delivering brilliant experiences for our guests and creating opportunities for our teams. Together, they guide a growing, inclusive and ambitious business, focused on doing the right thing and bringing the heart of hospitality to life across Ireland, the UK and continental Europe.

###### Dermot Crowley

#### Dermot Crowley

Chief Executive Officer

Dermot was appointed as CEO in November 2021, having joined Dalata as Deputy CEO in 2012. He was instrumental in the IPO in 2014 and the €450m acquisition of the Moran Bewleys Hotels in 2015 and has led the development of the Group’s brands, the embedding of innovation culture and sustainability focus, and the significant acceleration in growth since his appointment as CEO.

Prior to joining Dalata, Dermot held senior positions in Ion Equity (and Irish Private Equity firm). Jurys Doyle hotel Group, Renault Ireland and Forte Hotels. From 2000 to 2006, he was Head of Acquisitions and Development of Jurys Doyle Hotel Group, where he led the rollout of the Jurys Inn brand in the UK.

Dermot is a Fellow of the Chartered Accountants Ireland, having trained in PwC, and holds a first-class honours Business degree from University College Cork.

×

###### Shane Casserly

#### Shane Casserly

Deputy Chief Executive Officer

Shane joined Dalata in 2014 as Head of Strategy and Development. He joined the board in January 2020 as Corporate Development Director and was appointed as Deputy CEO in September 2024. Shane has led the identification and delivery of key strategic asset acquisitions and developments for the company in Ireland and the UK, and has spearheaded Dalata’s move into Continental Europe.

Prior to joining Dalata, Shane held senior positions with private Equity firm Ion Equity, Jurys Doyle Hotel Group, Microsoft Europe and Musgrave Group. Within Jurys Doyle Hotel Group, from 2004 to 2007, his focus was on the rollout of the Jurys Inn brand in the UK, ultimately being appointed to Head of Acquisitions and Development in 2006.

Shane is a Fellow of the Chartered Accountants Ireland, having trained in PwC, and holds a Commerce degree from University College Cork.

×

###### Des McCann

#### Des McCann

Chief Operations Officer

Des joined Dalata in 2011 as a hotel General Manager. He was promoted to Group General Manager – Clayton Hotels in 2018 before being appointed as COO in January 2022. He was appointed to the Board of Dalata in January 2025. Since taking over as COO, Des has been instrumental in promoting a culture of innovation and driving operational efficiencies and enhancing guest experiences across the group’s properties

Prior to this, Des served as General Manager of the Clyde Hotel, The Ballsbridge Hotel, and Clayton Hotel Dublin Airport, bringing extensive hotel operations experience to his current position.

Des is a graduate of the National College of Ireland, has served on the Board of The Irish Hospitality Institute and is a former National Council member of the Irish Hotels Federation

×

###### Sean McKeon

#### Sean McKeon

Company Secretary and Head of Risk & Compliance

Sean joined Dalata in November 2007, shortly after the business was founded. He trained as a chartered accountant with Sligo-based Gilroy Gannon and over the following fifteen years worked with companies including Dunnes Stores, Keelings, Aer Rianta International and Diageo, based in Ireland, Spain and Russia. Sean is an MBA graduate of the UCD Michael Smurfit Graduate Business School.

×

###### Roma O’Connor

#### Roma O’Connor

Chief Marketing Officer

Roma was appointed as CMO in May 2022, when she joined Dalata. Roma has been instrumental in the brand repositioning of Dalata Hotel Group, Clayton Hotels and Maldron Hotels that took place in 2024, as well as driving the group’s digital transformation and implementing efficiencies across marketing processes. Prior to joining Dalata, Roma held senior marketing positions in the Bank of Ireland, McDonald’s, and GE, bringing over 20 years of experience with leading global brands. Roma holds an Honours Degree in Business from Dundee Business School and has served as the president of the Advertising Association of Ireland.

×

###### Dawn Wynne

#### Dawn Wynne

Chief People Officer

As Chief People Officer at Dalata Hotel Group, Dawn Wynne has been instrumental in building a high-performing, values-driven culture that underpins the company’s growth and success. Since joining Dalata in 2008, she has led transformative initiatives in talent development, leadership capability, and employee engagement, positioning Dalata as an employer of choice within the hospitality sector. A strategic and people-focused leader, Dawn is passionate about driving organisational excellence through the empowerment and development of teams, ensuring that Dalata’s people strategy continues to deliver sustainable business performance.

×

# COO Appointment and Board Committee Changes

Dalata Hotel Group PLC (DAL,DHG) Dalata Hotel Group PLC: COO Appointment and Board Committee Changes08-Dec-2021 / 15:00 GMT/BSTDissemination of a Regulatory Announcement, transmitted by EQS Group.The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.

Dalata Announces COO Appointment and

Board Committee Changes

ISE: DHG LSE: DAL

Dublin and London, 8 December 2021 | Dalata Hotel Group plc ("Dalata" or the "Group" or the "Company"), the largest hotel operator in Ireland with a growing presence in the United Kingdom, is pleased to announce the appointment of Des McCann as Chief Operating Officer effective 1 January 2022. He will work closely with Stephen McNally until he retires from his executive role on 28 February 2022.

Mr McCann joined the Group in 2011. He has extensive senior hotel operations experience, previously being General Manager of The Clyde Court Hotel, The Ballsbridge Hotel and Clayton Hotel Dublin Airport. He was appointed Group General Manager of Clayton Hotels in Ireland in November 2018. He is the current Chairman of the Failte Ireland Steering Committee on Sustainability & Innovation and a member of the National Council of the Irish Hotels Federation.

Due to a change in personal circumstances, Conal O Neill has decided not to take up the role of Chief Operating Officer on 1 January 2022, as previously announced. Conal will continue in his current position as Group General Manager.

Dermot Crowley, Dalata Hotel Group CEO, commented: "I am delighted to have someone of the calibre of Des to step into the COO role. Des has been instrumental in growing the reputation of Clayton Hotels on the island of Ireland over the last three years and has skilfully led the Clayton team through very challenging times since the start of the Pandemic in early 2020. I look forward to continuing to work alongside both Des and Conal in the years ahead."

Board Committee Changes

Following the recent appointment of Cathríona Hallahan and Gervaise Slowey to the Board, the Company also announces a number of changes to its committee structures and individual non-executive directors' responsibilities which will also take effect from 1 January 2022.

Cathríona Hallahan will join the Audit & Risk Committee and the ESG Committee. Gervaise Slowey will join the Remuneration Committee and the ESG Committee and take over as non-executive director with responsibility for workforce engagement.

Shane Casserly will replace Stephen McNally on the ESG Committee

Our newly appointed non-executive directors will bring a wealth of knowledge and experience to the roles they will take up on 1 January 2022, and I am looking forward to working with them in the years ahead. I am also looking forward to the contribution Shane Casserly will make as a member of the ESG committee. Shane will bring to this committee significant knowledge of ESG matters and a keen interest in embedding sustainability in the business. Shane will replace Stephen McNally on the ESG committee. Stephen, whose retirement from his executive role in early 2022 has already been announced, will retire from the Board on 31 December 2021. I would again like to acknowledge Stephen's immense contribution, as a director and as an executive, to the success of Dalata over many years, and to wish him well in his retirement."

About Dalata

Dalata Hotel Group plc was founded in August 2007 and listed as a plc in March 2014. Dalata has a strategy of owning or leasing its hotels and also has a small number of management contracts. The Group's portfolio now consists of 29 owned hotels, 12 leased hotels and three management contracts with a total of 9,166 bedrooms. In addition to this, the Group is currently developing 11 new hotels and has plans to extend three of its existing hotels, adding over 2,600 bedrooms. The new rooms will bring the total number of bedrooms in Dalata to over 11,800. For the first six months of 2021, Dalata reported revenue of €39.6 million and a loss after tax of €30.4 million. Dalata is listed on the Main Market of Euronext Dublin (DHG) and the London Stock Exchange (DAL). For further information visit: www.dalatahotelgroup.com

Des has been instrumental in growing the reputation of Clayton Hotels on the island of Ireland over the last three years and has skilfully led the Clayton team through very challenging times since the start of the Pandemic in early 2020. I look forward to continuing to work alongside both Des and Conal in the years ahead.”

#### Board Committee Changes

Following the recent appointment of Cathríona Hallahan and Gervaise Slowey to the Board, the Company also announces a number of changes to its committee structures and individual non-executive directors’ responsibilities which will also take effect from 1 January 2022.

Cathríona Hallahan will join the Audit & Risk Committee and the ESG Committee. Gervaise Slowey will join the Remuneration Committee and the ESG Committee and take over as non-executive director with responsibility for workforce engagement.

Shane Casserly will replace Stephen McNally on the ESG Committee

#### Board Committees with effect from 1 January 2022:

John Hennessy, Group Chair, commented: “I am delighted to announce these changes to our Board committees. Our newly appointed non-executive directors will bring a wealth of knowledge and experience to the roles they will take up on 1 January 2022, and I am looking forward to working with them in the years ahead. I am also looking forward to the contribution Shane Casserly will make as a member of the ESG committee. Shane will bring to this committee significant knowledge of ESG matters and a keen interest in embedding sustainability in the business. Shane will replace Stephen McNally on the ESG committee. Stephen, whose retirement from his executive role in early 2022 has already been announced, will retire from the Board on 31 December 2021. I would again like to acknowledge Stephen’s immense contribution, as a director and as an executive, to the success of Dalata over many years, and to wish him well in his retirement.”

# Carol Phelan
### CFOat[Dalata Hotel](https://theorg.com/org/dalata-hotel)
Contact
Carol Phelan is the Chief Financial Officer at Dalata Hotel. Prior to this role, Carol served as Associate Director at Ion Equity from 2007 to 2014. In this role, Carol was responsible for deal origination, evaluation, negotiation and execution for a variety of complex investment opportunities across multiple sectors. Additionally, Carol led post-acquisition assessment and re-organization efforts, as well as monitored investee performance and prepared reports for internal and external stakeholders. In one investee company, Carol even took on an interim senior executive role, leading the recruitment of a new CEO and Finance Director while also overhauling the company's financial analysis output. Carol's experience and drive for success has served their well in their current role at Dalata Hotel.
Carol Phelan has a Board Ready Programme from Deloitte & 30% Club Ireland, a Women Leaders Programme from INSEAD, and a Master in Accounting from UCD Michael Smurfit Graduate School of Business. Carol also has a Bachelor of Business Studies with French from University of Limerick and an Erasmus Programme from EM Normandie Business School.
Some direct reports include Tony McGuigan - Head of Procurement, Rachel Cafolla - Group Head of Financial Reporting & Treasury Manager, and Stephen Clarke - Group Financial Controller. Carol Phelan reports to Dermot Crowley, CEO. They are on a team with Patrice Lennon - Group Director of Sales, Niall Macklin - Head of Acquisitions, and Martha Mannion - Group Director of Rooms Revenue & Distribution Strategy.
